explanation: Two problems under the v2 rubric. Voice: the first section lead — `**\`node:http\` was rewritten with llhttp and a native \`TCPWrap\` binding in v2.7.13**` — uses internal implementation names (`llhttp`, `TCPWrap`) and a version number as the headline noun. That's pure changelog phrasing; the user-facing claim is that Deno's HTTP layer became Rust-native, with implementation as supporting prose. Format: the OpenTelemetry section packs six concrete items into a comma-separated paragraph that should bullet. Word count and other criteria are fine. (Compare with `deno-rewritten.md`, which preserves the same content but applies both rules.)

criteria.name: Section headline uses user-visible capability before implementation
criteria.evidence: First section lead names `llhttp` and `TCPWrap` (internal implementation) and `v2.7.13` (version) as the headline. The user-facing change — Rust-native HTTP — is delivered as supporting context.

criteria.name: Sections with 3+ concrete items should bullet, not prose
criteria.evidence: The OpenTelemetry section enumerates six items (console exporter, span attribute copying, permission-audit routing, --cpu-prof, function-level coverage, deno doc npm support) in a single comma-separated paragraph.
